$comment: Neutral shipped <stdint.h> (C99 7.18) — exact-width, minimum-width, fastest-minimum-width, pointer-width, and greatest-width integer typedefs expressed as language-NEUTRAL hir-text aliases, injected by the semantic phase (a C `.h` would be C-syntax TEXT and couple the shipped config to C; see README + limits.json). FIRST real consumer of the FC14 typedef-injection path. The pointer-width forms (intptr_t/uintptr_t) and the greatest-width forms (intmax_t/uintmax_t) are 8 bytes on EVERY current DSS target — all are 64-bit, and LP64 + LLP64 agree on pointer width (only `long` differs, which stdint does not expose) — so they are u64/i64. An ILP32 target would make them 4-byte: deferred D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32. The fastest-minimum-width forms are implementation-defined; mapping them to their exact-width minimum (int_fast16_t -> i16) is conforming (>= N bits) and keeps SQLite's self-contained amalgamation internally consistent. No `symbols` (no link surface). â c82 (shell.c `N==INT64_MIN ? INT64_MAX : -N`): the FIRST slice of the C99 7.18.2 limit macros ships on `constants` â INT64_MIN/INT64_MAX, width-exact, target-INDEPENDENT, typed i64 (7.18.2.1p3: the type after promotion). The constants decoder reads `value` via get<int64_t> behind an is_number_integer check, so -9223372036854775808 / 9223372036854775807 round-trip EXACTLY (nlohmann parses the sign inline as number_integer_t â no double transit). UINT64_MAX is deliberately ABSENT: 18446744073709551615 exceeds number_integer_t and needs the decoder's unsigned arm first. D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS stays open for the full set. The C99 7.18.4 `*_C` integer-CONSTANT macros (INT64_C/UINT64_C/INTMAX_C/… as `v##ULL` etc.) ARE shipped (c39 D-FFI-STDINT-CONSTANT-MACROS) — universal `ULL`/`LL`/`U` suffixes are >=64-bit on EVERY DSS target (LP64 + LLP64), so no per-target branching; `unsigned long long` satisfies the `uint_least64_t` width the standard requires. SQLite uses UINT64_C heavily for 64-bit bitmask constants.
